mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, tight labor markets compress wage growth and signal peak capacity utilization, triggering mean-reversion in equity prices over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2010 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and residential housing starts indicates broad-based economic acceleration, which historically precedes earnings upgrades and positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 150 basis points over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the cross-correlation between INDPRO and HOUST drops below 0.4.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery environment, rising real consumer credit balances reflect renewed household spending power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as corporate revenues expand.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when RSAFS growth exceeds 2.0 percent annually during recessionary periods, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 55 percent over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trajectory in the industrial production index signals expanding manufacturing capacity and improving supply-side efficiency, which compresses input costs and supports equity valuations over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns show no statistically significant correlation with INDPRO month-over-month changes, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 50 percent of trading days.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.25 percent, the Federal Reserve signals the end of the zero-lower-bound era, increasing discount rates and compressing equity multiples over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Equity valuations expand despite FEDFUNDS crossing above 0.25 percent, or the signal's information coefficient remains below 0.02 across all post-2008 periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in nonfarm payrolls growth and real consumer credit expansion indicates synchronized labor market strength and household balance sheet repair, which historically drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive alpha relative to a market-cap weighted benchmark over a 3-year walk-forward test, or the joint probability of both series rising concurrently drops below 30 percent.

mechanism_narrative: During a disinflationary growth regime, rising real GDP combined with stable consumer price indices reduces uncertainty premiums and supports equity risk appetite over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0 percent annually despite GDPC1 expansion, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50 percent in high-inflation environments.
